title: elaphoglossum latebricola
intro: Elaphoglossum latebricola is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, native to Costa Rica and Panama. The plant has been studied for its medicinal properties, including its antifungal and antibacterial activities, which have been attributed to its flavonoid and phenolic compounds. Elaphoglossum latebricola is believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its leaves, which can be dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions with other medications.
